proposals.title: P12 Oscillation: Enforce Revert on >20% Regression — Q3 Violated Twice
proposals.detect: Q3 iter_3 produced 178 ms vs. best 107 ms (+66%) and iter_5 produced 186 ms vs. best 91 ms (+105%). Both exceeded the 20% P12 threshold but the regressed iterations were still compiled and run. The `build_joins` phase oscillated between 35 ms and 119 ms across iterations, indicating the optimizer alternated between two join-build strategies rather than locking in the winner.
proposals.fix: When the optimizer records that an iteration is `improved: false` AND the regression exceeds 20% of the current best, the next iteration MUST start from the best-known source (not the most-recent source). Additionally, when `build_joins` timing regresses >2× from prior best while `main_scan` is stable, tag the change as a join-strategy flip and explicitly revert the join-ordering/sizing decision that caused it.
proposals.evidence: Q3: iter_3=178ms (+66% vs best 107ms), iter_5=186ms (+105% vs best 91ms). build_joins: 136→77→39→119→35→110 ms.

proposals.title: Unnecessary Join Build for Single-Table Aggregation Queries
proposals.detect: When `build_joins` phase consumes >30 ms but the query is a pure filter+aggregate on a single large table (e.g., Q6: filter LINEITEM by date/discount/quantity, then SUM), the join-build scaffolding is superfluous. In Q6 iter_0, build_joins was 82.7 ms (62% of total) with no actual join needed. This was eliminated in iter_1 reducing build_joins to 1.9 ms and achieving 6.3× speedup.
proposals.fix: Before generating join-build code, verify the query requires a join (multiple source tables in the FROM clause or correlated sub-query). For single-table aggregation queries with only range/equality predicates on the scan table, omit the build_joins phase entirely. If a pre-built hash index is loaded but not probed, do not mmap it.

proposals.title: dim_filter Dominance in Sub-Query Aggregation (HAVING on Large Table)
proposals.detect: When `dim_filter` accounts for >50% of total execution time and the query has a sub-query aggregating a large table (e.g., Q18: find orders where SUM(l_quantity) > 300), the filter phase is performing a full aggregation pass over LINEITEM. Q18 iter_0–iter_2 each spent ~100 ms in dim_filter (55% of total). iter_3 reduced it to 38.7 ms via a more efficient approach.
proposals.fix: For HAVING-style sub-queries over LINEITEM at SF10 (60M rows): (1) perform the aggregation in a single parallel pass with a thread-local hash map, then merge; (2) if a pre-built group-sum index exists for the aggregation key, use it instead of a full scan. When dim_filter >50% of total, this phase must be the first optimization target.

proposals.title: Cold mmap Start Inflates First-Iteration Timing — Use Warm Run for Optimization Baseline
proposals.detect: Q1 iter_0 shows mmap_open=114 ms vs. iter_1 mmap_open=0.6 ms — a 190× difference due to cold OS page cache. Q9 iter_0 total=244 ms vs. iter_1 total=112 ms (the ~80 ms gap is similarly from cold mmap). The iter_0 timing is not representative of steady-state performance.
proposals.fix: When establishing the optimization baseline, use the timing from iter_1 (first warm run) rather than iter_0 if mmap_open in iter_0 exceeds 20 ms. Alternatively, discard the mmap_open time from iter_0 when computing `timing_ms` for the baseline comparison. This prevents the optimizer from treating iter_1 as a spurious 2× improvement when it is merely cache-warm.

meta.notes: All queries produced correct results. No correctness bugs observed. Main issues are optimizer oscillation (especially Q3) and cold-start mmap inflation.
